Deine Mission


Der Er
description_full:
Bei Laserhub liegt Metall in unserer DNA und mit unserer Beschaffungsplattform stellen wir die traditionelle Metallindustrie ordentlich auf den Kopf!

Seit 2017 zeigt unser Expertenteam mit viel Leidenschaft, dass unser Ansatz im Markt ankommt. Jetzt gehen wir den nächsten Schritt: Wir wollen die gesamte Metallverarbeitungsindustrie digitaler, effizienter und zukunftsfähig machen.

Um dieses ambitionierte Ziel zu erreichen, suchen wir aktuell ein herausragendes neues Teammitglied als


Product Manager - Growth & Retention (m/w/d)

Als Product Manager verantwortest du einen zentralen Produktbereich end-to-end – von Discovery und Strategie über Priorisierung und Delivery bis hin zur kontinuierlichen Optimierung anhand echter Kund:innen- und Businessbedürfnisse.








Deine Mission


Der Erfolg des Produkts liegt in deiner Hand: Du leitest, mit einem hohen Maß an Eigenverantwortung, den gesamten Produktzyklus im Customer-Success-Bereich, von Discovery und Ideenfindung, über Priorisierung und Umsetzung, bis hin zur kontinuierlichen Weiterentwicklung.
Dein Arbeitsfeld umfasst unter anderem:
- Registrierung, Onboarding und Reaktivierung
- Transparenz & Nutzerbindung (Dashboards, Bestellungen, Reklamationen)
- Kundenbindungsmechanismen wie Loyalty-Systeme oder Teilebibliotheken
- Zahlungsinfrastruktur
- Analytics & Tracking
Die Priorisierung relevanter Pain-Points: Du identifizierst und evaluierst die wichtigsten Initiativen, indem du Kundenprobleme, Unternehmensziele, Markttrends und Wettbewerbsentwicklungen verstehst und einordnest.
Gemeinsam mit einem starken Team entwickelst du Lösungen: Du definierst Anforderungen, formulierst verständliche User Stories und entwickelst die Roadmap deines Bereichs weiter. Du arbeitest eng mit Entwickler:innen, Designer:innen und internen Expert:innen zusammen, um komplexe Probleme zu lösen, neue Ideen umzusetzen und bestehende Funktionen kontinuierlich zu verbessern.
Die Zufriedenheit der Kund:innen ist dein Fokus: Du stehst im regelmäßigen Austausch mit Kund:innen über verschiedene Kanäle und repräsentierst ihre Perspektive innerhalb des Unternehmens. Du bist verantwortlich für eine herausragende Customer Experience und echten Mehrwert.
Gemeinsam wachsen wir: Du analysierst die Produktperformance und relevante Kennzahlen, um sicherzustellen, dass Ziele erreicht werden. Deine Arbeit ist datenbasiert, du lernst schnell aus Ergebnissen und entwicklst Lösungen kontinuierlich weiter.
Du sorgst für einen erfolgreichen Stakeholder-Austausch: In enger Zusammenarbeit mit Marketing, Sales und weiteren Product-Teams, stellst du sicher, dass die richtigen Initiativen umgesetzt werden, erfolgreiche Produkteinführungen möglich sind und alle relevanten Teams abgestimmt zusammenarbeiten.






Dein Profil


Abgeschlossenes Studium in den Bereichen Ingenieurwesen, Wirtschaft, Softwareentwicklung, Marketing, Data/Analytics oder einem vergleichbaren Bereich
Mindestens 3 Jahre Erfahrung im Software Product Management sowie mit komplexen Web-Technologien – idealerweise im kundenorientierten Umfeld von Marktplätzen, E-Commerce, B2B-Plattformen oder anderen digitalen Plattformmodellen
Starkes Verständnis für Funnel-Analysen, Automatisierungen, kundenorientierte Optimierungen und Systemintegrationen
Sehr gute analytische Fähigkeiten sowie mindestens 2 Jahre Erfahrung mit Datenanalyse-Tools wie SQL, Excel, Datenvisualisierung/-transformation, Pivot-Tabellen oder vergleichbaren Lösungen
Technisches Grundverständnis sowie Interesse an modernen Technologien und AI-gestützten Arbeitsweisen sind ein Plus
Sehr gute Kommunikationsfähigkeiten in Deutsch und Englisch – sowohl schriftlich als auch mündlich
Begeisterung für komplexe digitale Produkte sowie für die Digitalisierung einer traditionellen Industrie
Eigenständige, pragmatische und lösungsorientierte Arbeitsweise mit hoher Eigeninitiative
Freude an einem schnellen, unternehmerischen Umfeld und daran, bestehende Prozesse kritisch zu hinterfragen






Deine Meilensteine


Nach 1 Monat: Du verstehst unser Produkt, die wichtigsten Herausforderungen und bringst dich bereits aktiv in die Weiterentwicklung von Lösungen ein
Nach 3 Monaten: Du bist ein wichtiger Teil unseres Product-Prozesses und wirkst aktiv an Priorisierung und Produktentscheidungen mit
Nach 6+ Monaten: Du kennst deinen Bereich im Detail, arbeitest vollständig eigenständig und gestaltest dessen zukünftige Entwicklung aktiv mit







Warum Laserhub?


Echter Impact statt reiner Feature-Delivery: Du entwickelst nicht einfach einzelne Features, sondern gestaltest aktiv mit, wie wir Kund:innen gewinnen, binden und langfristig begeistern
Ownership, die du wirklich spürst: Keine starren Prozesse, die du einfach abarbeitest. Du bekommst den Raum, Entscheidungen zu treffen, Dinge zu hinterfragen und Produkte nachhaltig besser zu machen

Internationales, modernes Arbeitsumfeld: Du arbeitest in einem englischsprachigen, diversen Team und bewegst dich täglich in einem internationalen B2B-Kontext.

Benefits, die zu deinem Leben passen: Ob monatlicher Voucher, Wellpass / EGYM oder andere Optionen, wir bieten dir Flexibilität statt Standardlösungen

Flexibilität, die wirklich gelebt wird: arbeite remote oder aus Stuttgart und gestalte deinen Arbeitsalltag so, dass er zu deinem Leben passt

Dynamik mit Substanz: Wir verbinden die Geschwindigkeit eines Tech-Startups mit einem Markt, der langfristig relevant ist





Über uns



Bei uns haben wir Blech im Blut, mit unserer Beschaffungsplattform für den Teilekauf mischen wir die sehr traditionelle Blechindustrie auf. Bei uns kommen Endkunden und Blechbearbeiter über einen schlanken, digitalen Prozess zusammen. Statt sich wie bisher Tage oder Wochen mit einem Auftrag zu beschäftigten, verläuft hier jeder unschlagbar effizient - davon haben beide Seiten etwas.

Mit viel Leidenschaft hat unser Expertenteam seit 2017 bewiesen, dass unser Prinzip am Markt ankommt - jetzt wollen wir daran mitwirken, die gesamte metallverarbeitende Industrie digital und zukunftsfähig zu gestalten. Um unser großes Ziel zu erreichen, sind wir aktuell auf der Suche nach exzellenten Mitarbeitern und freuen uns über deine Bewerbung.

… more

description_full:
With a passion for life

Join our diverse teams of passionate people and a career that allows you to develop both personally and professionally. At Getinge we exist to make life-saving technology accessible for more people. To make a true difference for our customers – and to save more lives, we need team players, forward thinkers, and game changers.

Are you looking for an inspiring career? You just found it.

We are a global leader in delivering innovative solutions that shape the future of surgical workflows, operating room efficiency, and patient safety. Our OR Solutions portfolio empowers hospitals and clinicians with technology that elevates performance, quality, and outcomes. As we continue to expand and innovate, we’re looking for a highly driven Product Manager to join our team and help shape the next generation of OR solutions.

Our OR Solutions portfolio consists of Cloud based Applications for Operating Room Scheduling, Media Management, OR Integration and OR Management and Patient Flow Management Solutions. The ongoing development of Torin is guided by the needs of international markets and regulatory requirements.

New features such as AI-powered prediction and analysis is implemented in close collaboration with users and partners in different countries. With Torin, we offer a future-proof platform that helps hospitals optimize processes, enhance patient safety, and ensure sustainable economic success.

About You

As  Product Manager – OR Solutions  , you take ownership of the product lifecycle from strategy to execution. You will collaborate closely with R&D, marketing, sales, regulatory, and clinical experts to define market needs and translate them into differentiated offerings. You are the voice of the customer, the advocate for innovation, and the orchestrator of successful product launches.

We are looking for a motivated, positive and results orientated Product Manager with experience working within product management. You are comfortable managing multiple projects simultaneously, prioritizing activities to support roadmap development and comfortable collaborating in a complex stakeholder environment. You are a strong collaborator who is accustomed to working in cross functional teams and can clearly articulate the problem and proposed solutions you have in mind. You have an ability to influence via diplomacy and tact.

You will be responsible for


Take ownership of the entire product lifecycle—from strategy and development to launch and continuous improvement.
Business performance of the product/solution from launch to discontinuation.
Collaborate closely with R\&D, Marketing, Sales, Regulatory, and clinical experts.
Identify and prioritize market and customer needs, translating them into differentiated offerings.
Lead user research and usability studies, working with designers, developers, and research teams.
Support go-to-market activities and sales for digital solutions.
Drive roadmap and release planning for digital products and solutions.
Monitor market trends and regulatory requirements to ensure competitiveness and compliance.
Present new product launches and upgrades to internal and external stakeholders.
Develop clear commercial guidelines and frameworks (e.g., pricing, commercial terms, legal agreements).
Evaluating and prioritizing business potential for the Torin Suite.
Internal and external presentation of new product launches or upgrade events.
Leading the definition of clear commercial guidelines and frameworks, i.e., pricing guidelines, commercial terms, legal agreements etc.


Your Experience


Bachelor’s degree and/or a 3+ years’ equivalent experience in product management; strategic marketing and technical experience a plus.
Strong understanding of clinical workflows, preferably in the operating room environment.
Experience with IT Solutions in hospitals in the Operating Room Area and services in health care or other industries is an advantage.
Experience in implementing roadmaps.
Experience presenting in larger forums.
Fluency in English written and spoken (Company language is English).


What We Offer


An international, innovative work environment
Diverse opportunities for professional development and training
Flexible working hours and remote work options
A committed, interdisciplinary team


This role will report to Director Product Management and Business Development for OR and PFM Solutions.

Business travel will be required in the role to visit internal and external customers.

About Us

With a firm belief that every person and community should have access to the best possible care, Getinge provides hospitals and life science institutions with products and solutions aiming to improve clinical results and optimize workflows. The offering includes products and solutions for intensive care, cardiovascular procedures, operating rooms, sterile reprocessing and life science. Getinge employs over 12,000 people worldwide and the products are sold in more than 135 countries.

Reasonable accommodations are available upon request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection process.

If you have any questions, please reach out to Natalia Bodirogic (natalia.bodirogic@getinge.com).

description_full:
About the Company

On behalf of our client, an international research and advisory firm helping businesses and investors understand markets, customers, and opportunities through high-quality data, research, and insights, we are looking for a Research Project Manager to contribute to the delivery of market research and analytical projects from start to finish, working closely with experienced colleagues, clients, and external collaborators. In this role, you will combine data analysis, project coordination, and market research, ensuring projects are executed efficiently while transforming research findings into meaningful insights for clients and stakeholders.




Your Day-to-Day

Plan and coordinate research activities to ensure projects stay on schedule
Conduct desk research and collect quantitative and qualitative data
Analyze datasets to identify market trends, patterns, and key insights
Prepare clear reports, presentations, and visualizations for clients and internal teams
Coordinate with data collectors, designers, and external partners
Support project timelines, budgets, and resource allocation
Address research-related queries from clients and internal stakeholders
Present findings during client meetings, webinars, or industry events
Assist in coordinating work with part-time contributors and external suppliers




Requirements

Academic background in Research, Economics, Business, Social Sciences, or a related field
Strong analytical skills and comfort working with data
Advanced proficiency in Excel and PowerPoint
Ability to manage multiple tasks and follow projects through to completion
Strong organizational and problem-solving skills
Excellent communication and presentation skills
Curiosity, initiative, and willingness to learn new tools and research methodologies
Ability to work effectively in a remote, international team environment




What’s in It for You

Opportunity to work on international market research projects impacting investment decisions
Exposure to real estate and rented residential market intelligence
Fully remote work environment with flexible collaboration
Collaborative and knowledge-driven team culture
Opportunity to grow your research, analytics, and project management skills
… more

Requirements added by the job poster

• 4+ years of Research Services experience

• 3+ years of work experience with Microsoft PowerPoint

• 3+ years of work experience with Microsoft Excel

description_full:
Your job at DKV Mobility? As part of a leading European B2B platform for on-the-road payment solutions, you will work in an exciting environment. Our tasks are defined by our Purpose: To drive the transition towards an efficient and sustainable future of mobility. We offer you numerous opportunities to help shape the mobility of tomorrow in a dynamic and agile environment. Our approximately 2,700 employees throughout Europe are united by a common passion: to give their best every day for our customers in more than 50 countries. True to our claim: You drive, we care. Become part of our diverse, dedicated and unique team!

What you can expect


You design and scale SaaS product operations across digital product teams, ensuring discovery‑led, user‑centric ways of working.
You establish standardized product discovery, tooling, and enablement frameworks that create consistency and scalability across products.
You lead ecosystem & partner management as well as digital financial planning and reporting, ensuring commercial alignment and transparency.
You empower Sales, Customer Service, and Marketing with product expertise and enablement to drive effective SaaS go‑to‑market motions.
You build up and lead a high‑performing team of Digital Product Operations and Business Development Managers while shaping a strong outcome‑, data‑, and experimentation‑driven product culture.


What makes you stand out


You hold a university degree in Business Administration, Economics, Engineering, or a comparable field; an MBA or equivalent is a plus.
You bring 5+ years of professional experience in digital product management, SaaS product operations, business development, or platform‑driven environments.
You have proven experience working with SaaS business models, including recurring revenue logic, unit economics, forecasting, and ROI‑based portfolio steering.
You have demonstrated success in building or scaling product operating models, discovery standards, and enablement frameworks across multiple product teams.
You have strong experience working with executive‑level stakeholders and can communicate complex topics clearly and in a decision‑oriented manner.


We are looking forward to your application and to applicants who enrich our diverse culture! Regardless of age, gender, origin and sexual identity and orientation. Severely disabled applicants will be given preference if equally qualified. This position can be full-time or part-time (min. 30h/week). DKV Mobility is looking for the best talents and knows that these are not always available on a full-time basis. Many of our employees work part-time or in flexible working models. Please talk to us about the flexibility you need during the application process.

Why DKV Mobility? Here are five good reasons...

At DKV Mobility, we are team players - because we are only successful together! #teamofteams

You should feel welcome with us from the very beginning!

We've Been Growing For About 90 Years

How we do it? We keep adapting our products and business model to meet changing customer needs. That's how we stay mobile and master every challenge the future holds.

There are also many other good reasons to work at DKV Mobility. For example, we offer you comprehensive company health management, parent-child offices (from 2023) and support you in leasing your eBike through our partner Jobrad!

We attach great importance to work-life balance.

We stand for the compatibility of family and career! That's why we offer flexible working hours and family-friendly working time models. In addition, we rely on a balanced mix of on-site meetings and home office.

We support you in your development

We offer you optimal conditions to familiarize yourself so that you can quickly find your way around and become a part of the team! To further your personal development, we offer you access to a modern learning environment - independent of time and location. This way you can also face the challenges of tomorrow!

We are committed to a net-zero future

After reaching our Green Pledge climate target in 2023 by reducing carbon emissions in our own business operations by 50.4% compared to the base year 2019, we set ourselves ambitious net-zero climate targets. The targets have been validated by the Science Based Targets initiative. By 2050, DKV Mobility commits to reach net-zero greenhouse gas emissions across the value chain from a 2022 base year. You want to take climate action? Follow us on our road to net-zero.

We are a certified employer

Our employees trust us. As an employer, we regularly receive awards from independent institutes, e.g. as Top Employer Germany and as Great Place to Work!

Have we made you curious? Then we are looking forward to your application! If you still have open questions then please contact Kartal Bilgic via kartal.bilgic@dkv-mobility.com.

description_full:
Über Dr. Willmar Schwabe

From Nature. For Health. Dr. Willmar Schwabe ist der weltweit führende Hersteller pflanzlicher Arzneimittel. Als Familienunternehmen mit über 150-jähriger Geschichte und weltweit rund 4.000 Mitarbeitenden stehen wir für Produkte von außergewöhnlich hoher Qualität. Um die Gesundheit von Menschen auf der ganzen Welt zu fördern, setzen wir auf moderne Forschung, Kundenorientierung sowie kompetente und motivierte Mitarbeitende.

It starts with nature. With you. Helfen Sie uns in Teilzeit mit 25 Wochenstunden, den Erfolg unserer Marketing- und Sales-Aktivitäten transparent zu machen und gezielt zu steuern als:

Marketing & Sales Controller (w/m/d)

Ort: Karlsruhe, hybrid

Firma: Dr. Willmar Schwabe

Bereich:  Commercial

Start: 01.10.2026

Arbeitszeitmodell: Teilzeit

Anstellungsart: unbefristetes Arbeitsverhältnis

Ihre Wachstum beflügelnden Aufgaben


Marketingcontrolling: Sie planen Absatz und Umsatz, erstellen Forecasts und unterstützen das Brand Management mit belastbaren Analysen.
Salescontrolling: Sie analysieren Kennzahlen des Praxis-Außendienstes, z. B. Besuchsfrequenz, Aktivitäten und Auslastung, und schaffen Transparenz für die Vertriebssteuerung.
Ergebnissteuerung: Sie betreuen die deutsche P&L im Master-Excel - von Ist-Zahlen über Planung und Forecast bis zur 4-Jahres-Planung.
Supply Chain Management: Sie berechnen Bestandsreichweiten, Produktionsmengen und begleiten Produktumstellungen bzgl. der Mengenplanung.
Dashboarding: Sie erstellen Dashboards in der SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) und bereiten Controlling-Daten adressatengerecht auf.
Schnittstellenmanagement: Sie arbeiten eng mit Brand Managern, Regionalleitern und weiteren Fachbereichen zusammen und bringen Ihre Analysen sicher in die Abstimmung ein.


Ihr natürlich überzeugendes Profil


Fachliche Anforderungen: Sie haben ein betriebswirtschaftliches Studium oder eine kaufmännische Ausbildung, z. B. als Industriekaufmann, abgeschlossen.
Erfahrung: Sie bringen erste Berufserfahrung im Controlling mit, idealerweise im Marketing- oder Sales Controlling. Sehr gute Excel-Kenntnisse, insbesondere Pivot, sowie Erfahrung mit BI-Tools setzen wir voraus, Kenntnisse in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) und mit Power Pivot sind von Vorteil.
Kompetenzen: Sie arbeiten zahlenaffin, analytisch und verstehen auch komplexe betriebswirtschaftliche Zusammenhänge schnell und sicher.
Persönliche Stärken: Sie arbeiten sorgfältig, eigenverantwortlich und engagiert. Für die Zusammenarbeit mit angrenzenden Bereichen bringen Sie das nötige Standing mit.
Sprachkenntnisse: Fließende Sprachkenntnisse in Deutsch runden Ihr Profil ab


Ihre von Natur aus wertschätzenden Vorteile


Tarifvertrag Chemie: tarifvertragliche Vergütung inkl. Weihnachts- und Urlaubsgeld sowie attraktive Zusatzleistungen wie Pflegezusatz-, Berufsunfähigkeitsversicherung und arbeitgeberfinanzierte Altersvorsorge
Work-Life-Balance: 25 Stunden Arbeitswoche, 30 Tage Urlaub plus flexibel fünf zusätzliche Freistellungstage oder Sonderzahlung bei einer 5-Tage-Wosche, Gleitzeit und die Möglichkeit zu flexiblen Arbeitszeiten
Gesundheit und Wohlbefinden: betriebsärztlicher Dienst, Betriebssportangebote (z.B. Yoga) und täglich frische Gerichte in unserer Kantine
Weiterbildung: Entwicklungsmöglichkeiten durch die Schwabe Academy sowie externe Anbieter
Sonstiges: Langzeitkonto z.B. zur Nutzung für ein Sabbatical, kostenlose Parkmöglichkeiten, Rabatte für Fitnessstudios, freizeitliche Aktivitäten und Shopping, familiäre Arbeitsatmosphäre mit der Sicherheit eines wachsenden Familienunternehmens


Weitere Informationen

Persönlicher Kontakt

Nora Feskens

Tel.: +49 721 4005-342.

Dr. Willmar Schwabe GmbH & Co. KG

Willmar-Schwabe-Str. 4 | 76227 Karlsruhe

description_full:
From a local pharmacy to an international pharmaceutical company: that's us, Wörwag Pharma.

Our mission: Together for a healty life. We help to prevent and treat diseases of our time. Every day, we are committed to giving everyone the chance to live a healthy life - and have been doing so for more than 50 years!

Would you like to actively support us with this plan? Then apply now, because we are looking for you to start as soon as possible as a

Global Senior Brand Manager (m/f/x)

What You Can Expect


Strategic & Portfolio Leadership: Define and drive global category and brand strategies, including the development of toolkits and end-to-end lifecycle management, while continuously optimizing the portfolio based on market insights, performance data, and emerging trends.
Market Opportunity Management: Monitor market dynamics, customer needs, and the competitive landscape to identify growth opportunities, anticipate risks, and translate findings into actionable strategic recommendations.
Global Alignment & Governance: Ensure close alignment with local markets in executing the global marketing strategy and serve as the central interface for local marketing teams as well as key headquarters functions on all brand-related topics.
Pricing & Revenue Steering: Lead pricing and revenue management through strategic oversight, functional guidance, and performance-driven decision-making.
Market & Customer Insight: Initiate and oversee market research to build a deep understanding of physicians, pharmacists, and consumers, using insights to strengthen brand, portfolio, and go-to-market strategies.
Execution Support & Performance Management: Steer and support local marketing activities in line with global priorities, brand KPIs, and best-practice standards to ensure excellent execution in all markets.
Best Practice & Continuous Improvement: Foster collaboration and facilitate the exchange of successful approaches across markets to continuously enhance marketing effectiveness and execution quality.


What You Bring


Education & Experience: University degree in business administration, natural sciences, or a comparable field, combined with at least 8 years of relevant brand marketing experience within the pharmaceutical or healthcare industry (FS, OTC, Rx), ideally in an international environment.
Industry Expertise: Strong understanding of the food supplement ecosystem and proven experience managing brands consistently across regions, channels, and customer touchpoints with a clear portfolio and brand equity focus.
Marketing Excellence: Comprehensive command of the marketing mix, particularly digital brand communication, with hands-on success in launching products and/or product lines.
Consumer & Commercial Mindset: Deep understanding of customer needs, motivations, and behaviours, with the ability to convert insights into effective brand and go-to-market strategies.
Leadership & Collaboration: Intercultural sensitivity and demonstrated success working with and leading international teams, while confidently managing agencies, external partners, and budgets.
Personal Strengths: Creative, proactive, and analytical approach combined with a strong hands-on mentality, high commitment, and a clear focus on execution excellence.
Languages & Mobility: Very good command of English, strong MS Office skills, and willingness to travel internationally as required.


We offer you


A dynamic, international environment with the opportunity to take on responsibility quickly and extensively, to contribute experience and knowledge and to implement ideas
Varied challenges, as well as exciting projects with a strong team spirit
Compensation in line with the market and your performance, as well as additional benefits which can be used flexibly according to your needs, e.g.,
Subsidy for direct insurance
Individual compensation options for days off, pension provisions or payout
Bonago vouchers for special occasions
Support for your general well-being and/or work-life balance
Health-promoting measures such as discounted conditions on over-the-counter (OTC) pharmacy products or health days
Flexible trust working hours as well as the possibility to work mobile up to 60% of your working time
Central company headquarters with free parking facilities, good public transport connections as well as mobility allowances
A wide range of opportunities for personal and professional development
Regular employee events

We are looking forward to receiving your application!
